text:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
55-year-old male (date of birth 1969) with known Stage IV (M1c) BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma, primary site right upper back, with prior documented hepatic, nodal, and subcutaneous metastatic involvement. Patient recently confirmed to have radiographic and clinical progression on dabrafenib/trametinib combination targeted therapy after approximately 8 months of treatment. Pre-treatment brain MRI was negative for intracranial disease at time of initial diagnosis. Imaging now requested to reassess for interval development of intracranial metastases prior to planned initiation of second-line combination immunotherapy with ipilimumab and nivolumab. History of elevated LDH (589 U/L at time of progression). Resistance biopsy and molecular profiling recently completed.

COMPARISON:
Prior MRI brain with and without contrast (baseline staging, performed approximately 8 months prior): No intracranial metastatic disease identified at that time.

TECHNIQUE:
Multiparametric MRI of the brain performed on a 3.0 Tesla magnet. Sequences obtained include: sagittal T1-weighted (pre- and post-gadolinium), axial T1-weighted MPRAGE (pre- and post-gadolinium), axial T2-weighted fast spin echo, axial FLAIR (fluid-attenuated inversion recovery), axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping, axial gradient echo susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI), and coronal FLAIR. Intravenous gadolinium-based contrast (gadobutrol, 0.1 mmol/kg) administered without complication. Standard three-plane post-contrast T1-weighted imaging obtained, including thin-slice high-resolution volumetric acquisition through the entire intracranial compartment. Total imaging time approximately 45 minutes. Adequate image quality. No motion artifact degradation.

FINDINGS:

BRAIN PARENCHYMA:
The cerebral hemispheres demonstrate normal gray-white matter differentiation bilaterally without focal signal abnormality on T1, T2, or FLAIR sequences. No cortical or subcortical lesions suspicious for metastatic deposit are identified in either hemisphere. No parenchymal signal change to suggest vasogenic edema, cytotoxic edema, or infiltrative process. The basal ganglia, thalami, internal and external capsules, and deep white matter tracts are unremarkable in signal and morphology. No lobar or regional volume loss beyond expected for the patient's stated age.

GADOLINIUM ENHANCEMENT:
Following intravenous gadolinium administration, no abnormal parenchymal enhancement is identified. No ring-enhancing, nodular, or gyral enhancing lesions are visualized anywhere in the supratentorial or infratentorial compartments. No evidence of blood-brain barrier disruption suspicious for active metastatic implants. The normal vascular structures enhance appropriately without pathological thickening or irregular enhancement.

POSTERIOR FOSSA AND CEREBELLUM:
The cerebellum demonstrates normal foliation and signal bilaterally. No cerebellar hemispheric or vermian lesions identified. No foci of restricted diffusion or abnormal enhancement in the posterior fossa. The brainstem — midbrain, pons, and medulla — is normal in contour, signal, and size. The fourth ventricle is normally positioned and patent.

LEPTOMENINGEAL COMPARTMENT:
Careful review of the post-contrast sequences including thin-slice MPRAGE and coronal FLAIR demonstrates no pathological leptomeningeal enhancement. The sulci, cisterns, and fissures are patent and do not demonstrate abnormal signal or enhancement to suggest leptomeningeal carcinomatosis or meningeal infiltration. No sulcal FLAIR signal abnormality. The cerebrospinal fluid spaces appear within normal limits.

VENTRICULAR SYSTEM AND MIDLINE STRUCTURES:
The lateral ventricles are symmetric, normal in size and configuration. The third and fourth ventricles are unremarkable. No hydrocephalus. No transependymal edema. The midline structures are in normal position without shift. The corpus callosum is intact.

EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES:
The extra-axial spaces are symmetric and not expanded. No subdural or epidural collections. No extra-axial masses. The falx cerebri and tentorium cerebelli are unremarkable.

CALVARIUM AND SKULL BASE:
The osseous structures of the calvarium, skull base, and sella turcica are intact without lytic, sclerotic, or expansile lesions to suggest bony metastatic involvement. The outer and inner cortical tables are preserved. The diploic space demonstrates normal fatty marrow signal. No destructive bone lesions identified.

SELLA AND PITUITARY GLAND:
The pituitary gland is normal in size and morphology for the patient's age. No sellar or suprasellar mass. The pituitary stalk is midline and of normal caliber. No abnormal enhancement within the sella. (Noted for baseline given planned pituitary-toxic immunotherapy.)

ORBITS AND OPTIC APPARATUS:
The orbits are incompletely assessed on this dedicated brain protocol. The optic nerves and chiasm, to the extent visualized, appear unremarkable without enlargement or enhancement.

CEREBROVASCULAR STRUCTURES:
The major intracranial arteries and dural venous sinuses are patent on the basis of flow-related signal and enhancement on post-contrast sequences. No evidence of large vessel occlusion, dural sinus thrombosis, or abnormal vascular structure. No cavernous malformation on susceptibility-weighted imaging. No microhemorrhage.

DIFFUSION-WEIGHTED IMAGING:
No foci of restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarction, high-grade tumor, or epidermoid lesion.

IMPRESSION:

1. No intracranial metastatic disease identified. Specifically, no parenchymal enhancing lesions, no leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal enhancement, and no calvarial or skull base involvement to suggest intracranial spread of the patient's known metastatic melanoma.

2. Stable normal appearance of the brain parenchyma, ventricular system, and extra-axial spaces compared to the prior brain MRI obtained at baseline staging approximately 8 months ago. No interval change.

3. The pituitary gland appears normal at this baseline pre-immunotherapy study, noted given the known risk of hypophysitis associated with planned ipilimumab-based therapy.

4. No acute intracranial process identified.

This study does not demonstrate central nervous system involvement by metastatic melanoma at the current time. The imaging findings are consistent with M1c (non-CNS) classification.

Electronically verified and signed by:
Department of Neuroradiology
[Attending Neuroradiologist]
